name: spring-boot-jpa
description: Java 21 + Spring Boot 3 backend with JWT auth and JPA, PostgreSQL persistence, Vite/React frontend, Testcontainers, multi-stage Docker.
included: Spring Boot 3.4 with Spring Security + JWT (HS256) login flow
Spring Data JPA + Hibernate, PostgreSQL driver, H2 for unit tests
Testcontainers (Postgres) for integration tests with graceful Docker-unavailable fallback
Vite + React 19 + TypeScript frontend with RTK Query and react-router
Sample public + protected endpoints (/api/hello, /api/hello/me) with corresponding HomePage
Multi-stage Dockerfile: frontend build → backend build → JRE-only runtime image
selection_hints: Spring Boot REST API with PostgreSQL persistence
Full-stack app with Java backend and React frontend
Stateful backend service with relational database
anti_hints: Non-JVM language preference (Python, Go, Rust, Node)

verification.description: Compile Java + frontend, run unit tests on both sides, smoke-boot the Spring application context. `all` is the gate — exit 0 means the customised scaffold is healthy.

primary_files: src/main/java/com/example/app/Application.java — adjust SecurityFilterChain matchers for your routes
src/main/java/com/example/app/security/InMemoryUserStore.java — swap for a real UserDetailsService backed by your DB
src/main/java/com/example/app/security/JwtUtil.java — load the signing secret from env, not the hardcoded value
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx — replace with your real UI
frontend/src/api/api.ts — add endpoints for your routes
pom.xml — set groupId / artifactId / version to your project's coordinates
src/main/resources/application.properties — point at your real DB
docker-compose.yml — adjust ports, container names, env
preserve: Dockerfile — multi-stage build pattern is sound; modify only if you change the build commands
